revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '1941年、東京生まれ。横浜国立大学教育学部教育学科卒業。横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同...'
revision.text: 1941年、東京生まれ。横浜国立大学教育学部教育学科卒業。横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から横浜市役所、横浜児童相談所のソーシャルワーカーを務める。1991年、横浜市立大学国際文化学部教授として、社会福祉論を担当。2002年より、沖縄大学教授となり、2007年よりこども文化学科に所属する。沖縄大学人文科学部教授、沖縄子ども研究会会員。

{{Wikify}} 1941年、東京生まれ。横浜国立大学教育学部教育学科卒業。横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から横浜市役所、横浜児童相談所のソーシャルワーカーを務める。1991年、横浜市立大学国際文化学部教授として、社会福祉論を担当。2002年より、沖縄大学教授となり、2007年よりこども文化学科に所属する。[[沖縄大学]]人文科学部教授、沖縄子ども研究会会員。

typo

revision.text:
'''野本 三吉'''（のもと さんきち、[[1941年]][[11月30日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[教育学者]]、評論家、ノンフィクション作家、[[横浜市立大学]]名誉教授、[[沖縄大学]]学長。東京出身。本名・加藤彰彦。1964年[[横浜国立大学]][[教育学部]][[教育学科]]卒業。沖縄子ども研究会会員。
== 経歴 == 大学卒業後、横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から[[横浜市]]役所、横浜児童相談所のソーシャルワーカーを務める。横浜市立大学国際文化学部助教授、91年教授。社会福祉論を担当。2002年沖縄大学教授、2007年こども文化学科教授。2012年学長。
==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 c1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』筑摩書房・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』フレーベル館 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』明石書店 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』現代書館 2010
===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991
==参考== *『現代日本人名録2002』日外アソシエーツ *[http://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/soumuGakuchoAisatsu.php 沖縄大学]

revision.comment: スペース調整等

'''野本 三吉'''（のもと さんきち、[[1941年]][[11月30日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[教育学者]]、評論家、ノンフィクション作家、[[横浜市立大学]]名誉教授、[[沖縄大学]]学長。東京出身。本名・加藤彰彦。1964年[[横浜国立大学]][[教育学部]][[教育学科]]卒業。沖縄子ども研究会会員。
== 経歴 == 大学卒業後、横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から[[横浜市]]役所、横浜児童相談所のソーシャルワーカーを務める。横浜市立大学国際文化学部助教授、1991年教授。社会福祉論を担当。2002年沖縄大学教授、2007年こども文化学科教授。2012年学長。
==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』筑摩書房・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』フレーベル館 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』明石書店 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』現代書館 2010
===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991

'''野本 三吉'''（のもと さんきち、[[1941年]][[11月30日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[教育学者]]、評論家、ノンフィクション作家、[[横浜市立大学]]名誉教授、[[沖縄大学]]学長。東京出身。本名・'''加藤彰彦'''。1964年[[横浜国立大学]][[教育学部]][[教育学科]]卒業。沖縄子ども研究会会員。
== 経歴 == 大学卒業後、横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から[[横浜市]]役所、横浜児童相談所のソーシャルワーカーを務める。横浜市立大学国際文化学部助教授、1991年教授。社会福祉論を担当。2002年沖縄大学教授、2007年こども文化学科教授。2012年学長。2014年に退任。
==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』筑摩書房・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』フレーベル館 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』明石書店 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』現代書館 2010
===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991
==評伝== *『命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方』[[大倉直]] [[現代書館]] 2014
==参考== *『現代日本人名録2002』日外アソシエーツ *http://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/276

'''野本 三吉'''（のもと さんきち、[[1941年]][[11月30日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[教育学者]]、評論家、ノンフィクション作家、[[横浜市立大学]]名誉教授、前[[沖縄大学]]学長。東京出身。本名・'''加藤彰彦'''。1964年[[横浜国立大学]][[教育学部]][[教育学科]]卒業。沖縄子ども研究会会員。
== 経歴 == 大学卒業後、横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から[[横浜市]]役所、横浜児童相談所のソーシャルワーカーを務める。横浜市立大学国際文化学部助教授、1991年教授。社会福祉論を担当。2002年沖縄大学教授、2007年こども文化学科教授。2012年学長。2014年に退任。
==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』筑摩書房・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』フレーベル館 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』明石書店 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』現代書館 2010
===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991

==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』[[筑摩書房]]・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』[[フレーベル館]] 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』[[明石書店]] 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』[[現代書館]] 2010 *『貧困児童 子どもの貧困からの脱出』創英社・[[三省堂書店]] 2016
===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991
==評伝== *『命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方』[[大倉直]] [[現代書館]] 2014
== 参考 == * 『現代日本人名録2002』日外アソシエーツ * [http://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/276 沖縄大学による紹介]

'''野本 三吉'''（のもと さんきち、[[1941年]][[11月30日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[教育学者]]、評論家、ノンフィクション作家、[[横浜市立大学]][[名誉教授]]、前[[沖縄大学]]学長、同大名誉教授。[[東京都]]出身。本名'''加藤 彰彦'''（かとう あきひこ）。1964年[[横浜国立大学]][[教育学部]][[教育学科]]卒業。沖縄子ども研究会会員。
== 経歴 == 大学卒業後、横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から[[横浜市]]役所、横浜児童相談所のソーシャルワーカーを務める。横浜市立大学文理学部助教授を経て、1991年に教授。1995年より同大国際文化学部教授。社会福祉論を担当した。2002年沖縄大学教授、2012年～2014年に学長をつとめた。
==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』[[筑摩書房]]・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』[[フレーベル館]] 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』[[明石書店]] 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』[[現代書館]] 2010 *『貧困児童 子どもの貧困からの脱出』創英社・[[三省堂書店]] 2016
===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991
==評伝== *『命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方』[[大倉直]] [[現代書館]] 2014
== 参考 == * 『現代日本人名録2002』日外アソシエーツ * [http://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/276 沖縄大学による紹介]

'''野本 三吉'''（のもと さんきち、[[1941年]][[11月30日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[教育学者]]、評論家、ノンフィクション作家、[[横浜市立大学]][[名誉教授]]、前[[沖縄大学]]学長、同大名誉教授。[[東京都]]出身。本名'''加藤 彰彦'''（かとう あきひこ）。1964年[[横浜国立大学]][[教育学部]][[教育学科]]卒業。沖縄子ども研究会会員。
== 経歴 == 大学卒業後、横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から[[横浜市]]役所職員となり，寿生活館の相談員、児童相談所の児童福祉司（ソーシャルワーカー）を務める。横浜市役所を退職後、横浜市立大学文理学部助教授に就任。1991年に教授。1995年より同大国際文化学部教授。社会福祉論を担当した。2002年沖縄大学教授となり、2012年～2014年に学長をつとめた。
==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』[[筑摩書房]]・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』[[フレーベル館]] 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』[[明石書店]] 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』[[現代書館]] 2010 *『貧困児童 子どもの貧困からの脱出』創英社・[[三省堂書店]] 2016
===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991
==評伝== *『命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方』[[大倉直]] [[現代書館]] 2014
== 参考 == {{No footnotes|date=2018年4月|section=1}} * 『現代日本人名録2002』日外アソシエーツ * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ 沖縄大学による紹介]

===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991
==評伝== *『命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方』[[大倉直]] [[現代書館]] 2014
== 参考 == {{No footnotes|date=2018年4月|section=1}} * 『現代日本人名録2002』日外アソシエーツ * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ 沖縄大学による紹介]

revision.comment: 加筆及び存命人物の出典明記を追加。
revision.text:
{{存命人物の出典明記|date=2024-01}} '''野本 三吉'''（のもと さんきち、[[1941年]][[11月30日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[教育学者]]、評論家、ノンフィクション作家、[[横浜市立大学]][[名誉教授]]、前[[沖縄大学]]学長、同大名誉教授。[[東京都]][[本所区]](現[[墨田区]])出身<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[東京新聞]]|date=2023年7月7日|accessdate=2024-1-21}}</ref>。本名'''加藤 彰彦'''（かとう あきひこ）。1964年[[横浜国立大学]][[教育学部]][[教育学科]]卒業。沖縄子ども研究会会員。
== 経歴 == 大学卒業後、横浜市内の小学校教員を経て、日本列島を4年余り放浪し共同体巡りをする。1972年から[[横浜市]]役所職員となり、寿生活館の相談員、児童相談所の児童福祉司（ソーシャルワーカー）を務める。横浜市役所を退職後、横浜市立大学文理学部助教授に就任。1991年に教授。1995年より同大国際文化学部教授。社会福祉論を担当した。2002年沖縄大学教授となり、2012年～2014年に学長をつとめた。学長退任後はかつて職員として活動していた横浜市に戻り、[[中区 (横浜市)|中区]][[寿町 (横浜市)|寿町]]で街の歴史をまとめる活動に努めている<ref name="tokyo newspaper"/>。
==著書== *『不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡』社会評論社 1970 *『爆破 人間原型論序説』青林社 1971 *『いのちの群れ』社会評論社 1972 *『裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども』田畑書店 1974 *『太陽の自叙伝』柏樹社 1976 *『寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道』田畑書店 1977 *『個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信』増補改訂版 社会評論社 1979 *『戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて』協同出版 1981 教職課程新書 *『親とは何か N子への手紙』[[筑摩書房]]・ちくま少年図書館 1982 *『風の自叙伝』新宿書房 1982 *『空にでっかい雲がわく』[[フレーベル館]] 1983 いま、子どもたちは *『地域からの教育づくり 親とは何か、学校とは何か』矢田同和教育推進協議会学習パンフ 1983 *『いのちの群れ 共生・共死の原像』社会評論社 1985 *『子どものいる風景 孤立から自立への闘い』国土社の教育選書 1986 *『風になれ!子どもたち 児童ケースワーカー・十年の記録から』新宿書房 1992 *『近代日本児童生活史序説』社会評論社 1995 *『'''野本三吉ノンフィクション選集'''』全6巻 新宿書房 1996-2004 *『社会福祉事業の歴史』[[明石書店]] 1998 *『子ども観の戦後史』現代書館 1999 *『父親になるということ』海竜社 1999 *『生きる場からの発想 民衆史への回路』社会評論社 2001 *『生活者 野本三吉個人誌 1972-2002』第1~3分冊 社会評論社 2003 *『海と島の思想 琉球弧45島フィールドノート』現代書館 2007 *『沖縄・戦後子ども生活史』[[現代書館]] 2010 *『貧困児童 子どもの貧困からの脱出』創英社・[[三省堂書店]] 2016 *『まちに暮らしの種子(たね)を蒔く いま、この時代を生き抜くために』社会評論社 2018<ref name="tokyo newspaper"/> *『水滴の自叙伝 コミューン、寿町、沖縄を生きて』現代書館 2023<ref name="tokyo newspaper"/> ===共編著=== *『教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり』編集 筑摩書房 1981 *『21世紀の子どもと教育』編著 社会評論社 1985 *『現代子ども・若者考 自分さがしのジレンマ』石川恵美子共編 明石書店 1991
==評伝== *『命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方』[[大倉直]] [[現代書館]] 2014

{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. {{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />

Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.

revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.

{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />

In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />

Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). {{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />

Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />

{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} {{Academic-bio-stub}}

revision.comment: removing bad jawiki links
revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated close]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />

{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年qq月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.

== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==

After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

{{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941年11月30日}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|ja|横浜市立大学}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|ja|沖縄大学}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida City|ja|墨田区}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|ja|横浜国立大学}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
