He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|en|Yokohama National University|WD=Q1192619|lt=Yokohama National University}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobuki-cho|1=Kotobuki-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10871445}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Tadashi|1=Choku Ōkura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432441}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野本三吉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野本三吉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野本三吉]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1941|11|30}}|birth_place={{ill|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|teacher|WD=Q37226|1=Teacher}}|native_name=野本三吉}} {{Use dmy dates|date=January 2024}} '''Nomoto Sankichi''' (野本 三吉, {{ill|Nomoto Sankichi|ja|のもと さんきち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomoto Sankichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, born {{ill|November 30, 1941|ja|1941st year 11月30日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 30, 1941|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese educator, critic, non-fiction writer, and professor emeritus of {{ill|Yokohama City University|en|Yokohama City University|WD=Q914024|lt=Yokohama City University|1=Yokohama City University}}. He is a former president and professor emeritus of {{ill|Okinawa University|en|Okinawa University|WD=Q4617521|lt=Okinawa University|1=Okinawa University}}. He was born in {{ill|Honjo-ku|ja|本所区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honjo-ku|1=Honjo-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520362}}, now part of {{ill|Sumida, Tokyo|en|Sumida, Tokyo|WD=Q235135|lt=Sumida City|1=Sumida, Tokyo}}, Tokyo.<ref name="tokyo newspaper">{{Cite Web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/261437|title=横浜･寿町、沖縄を生きて…「人生の卒業論文」という自叙伝を出版 ペンネーム 野本三吉こと加藤彰彦さん（81）|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2023-07-07|access-date=2024-01-21}}</ref> His real name is '''Kato Akihiko''' (加藤 彰彦). He graduated from the Faculty of Education at {{ill|Yokohama National University|en|Yokohama National University|WD=Q1192619|lt=Yokohama National University|1=Yokohama National University}} in 1964 and is a member of the Okinawa Children's Research Society.
== Career == After graduating from university, Nomoto worked as an elementary school teacher in Yokohama. He spent over four years traveling across Japan, visiting various communes. In 1972, he joined the Yokohama City government, serving as a counselor at {{ill|Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja|寿生活館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobuki Seikatsu-kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and as a social worker at a child consultation center. After leaving his municipal position, he became an associate professor at Yokohama City University, where he later became a professor in 1991 and taught social welfare theory at the International Cultural Studies Department from 1995. In 2002, he joined Okinawa University as a professor and served as its president from 2012 to 2014. Following his tenure as president, Nomoto returned to Yokohama, where he has been involved in documenting the history of {{ill|Kotobuki-cho|ja|寿町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobuki-cho|1=Q10871445|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10871445}}, Yokohama.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
== Publications == * *''Fukashi no Kyōmyūn: Jiko Kyōiku no Sokuseki''* (不可視のコミューン 自己教育の足跡). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1970. * *''Bakuhatsu: Ningen Genkeiron Josetsu''* (爆破 人間原型論序説). Seirinsya, 1971. * *''Inochi no Mure''* (いのちの群れ). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1972. * *''Hadashi no Genjintachi: Kotobuki Chiku no Kodomo''* (裸足の原始人たち 寿地区の子ども). Tabata Shoten, 1974. * *''Taiyō no Jijoden''* (太陽の自叙伝). Hakujusha, 1976. * *''Kotobuki Seikatsukan Nōto''* (寿生活館ノート 職場奪還への遠い道). Tabata Shoten, 1977. * *''Kojin-shi Seikatsusha''* (個人誌・生活者 横浜・寿地区からの通信). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1979. * *''Sengo Jidō Seikatsu-shi''* (戦後児童生活史 子どもたちの原像を求めて). Kyōdō Shuppan, 1981. * *''Oya to wa Nani ka''* (親とは何か). Chikuma Shobō, 1982. * *''Kaze ni Nare! Kodomo-tachi''* (風になれ!子どもたち). Shinjuku Shobō, 1992. * *''Okinawa Sengo Kodomo Seikatsu-shi''* (沖縄・戦後子ども生活史). Gendaishokan, 2010. * *''Machi ni Kurashi no Tane o Maku''* (まちに暮らしの種子を蒔く). Shakai Hyōronsha, 2018.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" /> * *''Suiteki no Jijoden''* (水滴の自叙伝). Gendaishokan, 2023.<ref name="tokyo newspaper" />
=== Co-edited Works === * *''Kyōiku Jissen no Kiroku 8: Chiiki kara no Kyōiku-zukuri''* (教育実践の記録 8 地域からの教育づくり). Chikuma Shobō, 1981. * *''21 Seiki no Kodomo to Kyōiku''* (21世紀の子どもと教育). Shakai Hyōronsha, 1985.
== Biography == * *''Inochi no Tabibito: Nomoto Sankichi to Iu Ikikata''* (命の旅人 野本三吉という生き方). By {{ill|Ōkura Tadashi|ja|大倉直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Tadashi|1=Choku Ōkura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432441}}. Gendaishokan, 2014.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=April 2018|section=1}} * *Gendai Nihonjin Meiroku 2002*. Nichigai Associates. * [https://www.okinawa-u.ac.jp/about/professor/kato-akihiko/ Okinawa University profile]
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} {{Academic-bio-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}
